summary.root_cause: The live PostgREST schema cache still does not recognize pick_candidates.sport_key during upsert. Board-scan was already sending sport_key, and the DB-layer serializer always included the field even during retry by normalizing an omitted property to null, so the fallback path re-triggered the same schema-cache failure.
summary.resolution: Board-scan now detects sport_key schema-cache drift explicitly, logs a schema_cache_drift_fallback event, retries once with sport_key omitted at the DB payload boundary, and tags provenance with schema_cache_fallback metadata. Generated DB types now also include pick_candidates.sport_key so local type truth matches the intended schema.

commands: inline tsx runtime proof: runBoardScan -> runCandidateScoring -> runRankedSelection -> runBoardConstruction

out_of_scope: The upstream PostgREST schema cache itself is still stale for pick_candidates.sport_key; the runtime now handles that explicitly instead of failing closed.

Candidate scoring emitted experiment_ledger constraint warnings during proof (shadow_comparison_failed on experiment_ledger_run_type_check), but scoring continued and this lane does not change scoring or experiment-ledger contracts.
Pipeline health still reports 5 pending rows outside worker targets; worker targets remain healthy and this lane does not touch delivery routing.
